---
title: "Harmonized data cleanup"
output: pdf_document
author: "Savanah Leidholt"
date: "2026-02-05"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(dplyr)
library(haven)
library(SeuratObject)
```


#---------------------------------------------------
# inputting all directories and storage locations
#---------------------------------------------------
```{r}
#Directories
computer <- "mac studio"
#computer <- "mac laptop"
if (computer == "mac studio") {
  user <- Sys.info()[["user"]]
  if (user == "sleidholt") {
    dir.dat <- c("/Users/Shared/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/")
    dir.results <- c("/Users/Shared/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/Savanah Leidholt/")
    git_path <- "/Users/sleidholt/Documents/GitHub/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad/PANTHER PFAS/"
  }
} else {
  user <- Sys.info()[["user"]]
  if (user == "savanahleidholt") {
    dir.dat <- c("/Users/savanahleidholt/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/")
    dir.results <- c("/Users/savanahleidholt/OneDrive - UW/Laura Pyle's files - Biostatistics Core Shared Drive/Savanah Leidholt/")
    git_path <- "/Users/savanahleidholt/Desktop/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad"
  } 
}
```

```{r}
# ------------
#load objects 
#------------
harm <- readRDS(file.path(dir.dat, "Data Harmonization", "Data Clean", "harmonized_dataset.rds"))
dict <- readRDS(file.path(dir.dat, "Data Harmonization", "Data Clean", "data_dictionary_master.rds"))
harm_soma <- read.csv(file.path(dir.dat, "Data Harmonization", "Data Clean", "Soma_harmonized_dataset.csv"))
load(file.path(dir.dat, "PANTHER", "Data_Cleaned","panther_data_022326.Rdata")) #will load as panther_data
```


```{r}
#--------------------------
#reading in attempt
#--------------------------
attempt <- readRDS(file.path(dir.dat, "Savanah Leidholt", "T1D-T2D", "Data", "attempt_dat.rds"))

#trasnforming data to match the harmonized data
attempt_acr <- attempt %>%
  filter(visit == 0) %>%  # keep baseline rows
  mutate(
    visit = "baseline",   # recode 0 to match your harm_metab visit label
    acr_u_attempt = emu_urine_acr_mean * 8.84
  ) %>%
  select(subject_id, visit, acr_u_attempt)

 
panther_hba1c <- panther %>%
  select(record_id, visit, hba1c)
  
```


```{r}
#-----------------------------------------------
# bind attmempt & panther data to larger harmonized dataset
#-----------------------------------------------
attempt_acr$subject_id <- as.character(attempt_acr$subject_id)
panther_hba1c$record_id <- as.character(panther_hba1c$record_id)

harm <- harm %>%
  left_join(
    attempt_acr,
    by = c("record_id" = "subject_id", "visit" = "visit")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    acr_u = if_else(
      study == "ATTEMPT" & is.na(acr_u),
      acr_u_attempt,
      acr_u
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-acr_u_attempt)


# 2) Make sure harm$hba1c is numeric too (if it exists)
harm$visit <- as.character(harm$visit)
panther_hba1c$visit <- as.character(panther_hba1c$visit)

harm <- harm %>%
  left_join(panther_hba1c, by = c("record_id", "visit")) %>%
  mutate(
    hba1c = if_else(
      study == "PANTHER" & is.na(hba1c),
      hba1c_panther,
      hba1c
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-hba1c_panther)
```

```{r}
#-------------------------------------------------------------------
#load in data dictionary and create a lipids list and metabolites list
#-------------------------------------------------------------------

dict <- read.csv("/mmfs1/gscratch/togo/leidholt/Data/data_dictionary_master.csv")

# Subsetting for variables and creating a new dataframe
metab_bl_tiss <- dict[grepl("^metabolomics", dict$form_name), c("variable_name", "label", "form_name")]
metab <- metab_bl_tiss$variable_name
#metab_urine <- dict[grepl("^az_urine", dict$form_name), c("variable_name", "label", "form_name")] --urine metabolites are not found in harm_dataset so don't run this

lipids <- dict[grepl("lipidomics", dict$form_name), c("variable_name", "label", "form_name")] #lipids more straight forward
lip <- lipids$variable_name
```




```{r}
#-----------------
#bind metabolomics 
#-----------------

#Checking if any metabolites listed are not in the larger dataset---three don't exist in the larger dataset
setdiff(metab, colnames(harm))

#Checking to see if it's a case issue
metab_lower <- tolower(metab)
harm_cols_lower <- tolower(colnames(harm))

setdiff(metab, colnames(harm)) #still doesn't work so not a case issue proceed to only collect metabolites that intersect with larger data set

#only keep metabolites that actually exist in harm
metab_present <- intersect(metab, colnames(harm))

#studies I want to keep creating a variable name to a list
studies_keep <- c("ATTEMPT","RENAL-HEIR","RENAL-HEIRitage","PANTHER","CROCODILE","IMPROVE")

#same with ids columns (all categorical)
id_cols <- c(
  "record_id","mrn","attempt_id","croc_id","improve_id","rh_id","rh2_id","panther_id",
  "study","visit","group","sex","race","ethnicity"
)

#clinical numeric data I want to keep stored in a list
clin_cols <- c(
  "age","bmi","eGFR_fas_cr","hba1c",
  "acr_u"
)


#filtering metabolomics data
 # for non-numeric: keep value only if it's consistent within record_id+visit
 # for numeric: mean within record_id+visit (not counting NAs within the mean)
harm_metab <- harm %>%
  arrange(record_id) %>%
  filter(visit == "baseline") %>%
  filter(study %in% studies_keep) %>%
  select(any_of(c(id_cols, clin_cols)), any_of(metab)) %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      where(~ !is.numeric(.x)),
      ~{
        vals <- unique(na.omit(.x))
        if (length(vals) == 0) NA_character_
        else if (length(vals) == 1) as.character(vals)
        else NA_character_
      }
    ),
    across(
      where(is.numeric),
      ~ if (all(is.na(.x))) NA_real_ else mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)
    ),
    .by = c(record_id, visit)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    race_ethnicity_condensed = case_when(
      race == "White" & ethnicity == "Not Hispanic or Latino" ~ "Not Hispanic or Latino White",
      race == "Black or African American" & ethnicity == "Not Hispanic or Latino" ~ "Not Hispanic or Latino Black",
      ethnicity == "Hispanic or Latino" ~ "Hispanic or Latino",
      TRUE ~ "Not Hispanic or Latino Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  relocate(all_of(metab_present), .after = last_col())


#keep all NA MRNs -- unique patients involved in only one study
na_mrn <- harm_metab %>%
  filter(is.na(mrn))
  
#keep only one row per unqiue MRN--making sure patients only show up in one study
unique_mrn <- harm_metab %>%
  filter(!is.na(mrn)) %>%
  distinct(mrn, .keep_all = TRUE)

#combined unique ids
harm_metab <- bind_rows(na_mrn, unique_mrn)

#adding back in group info for attempt. For some reason it drops some of the Type 1 Diabetes group designations
harm_metab <- harm_metab %>%
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      grepl("ATTEMPT", study) & is.na(group) ~ "Type 1 Diabetes",
      TRUE ~ group
    )
  )

#subsetting data into data matrix and variable matrix
meta_metab <- harm_metab %>% select(1:20) 
metabolites <- harm_metab %>% select(-(1:20))

# ---- set rownames consistently for meta ----
meta_metab <- as.data.frame(meta_metab)
rownames(meta_metab) <- meta_metab$record_id
# ---- only do the above once ---------------

metabolites <- as.data.frame(metabolites)
rownames(metabolites) <- meta_metab$record_id

metabolites$leucyl_tryptophan_cl_tissue <- as.numeric(metabolites$leucyl_tryptophan_cl_tissue)

non_numeric <- names(metabolites)[!vapply(metabolites, is.numeric, logical(1))]
length(non_numeric)
head(non_numeric, 50)   # show first 50 offenders
```


```{r}
#-----------------------
#binding lipid data
#-----------------------

harm_lipids <- harm %>%
  arrange(record_id) %>%
  filter(study %in% studies_keep) %>%
  select(any_of(c(id_cols, clin_cols, lip))) %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      where(~ !is.numeric(.x)),
      ~{
        vals <- unique(na.omit(.x))
        if (length(vals) == 0) NA_character_
        else if (length(vals) == 1) as.character(vals)
        else NA_character_
      }
    ),
    across(
      where(is.numeric),
      ~ if (all(is.na(.x))) NA_real_ else mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)
    ),
    .by = c(record_id, visit)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    race_ethnicity_condensed = case_when(
      race == "White" & ethnicity == "Not Hispanic or Latino" ~ "Not Hispanic or Latino White",
      race == "Black or African American" & ethnicity == "Not Hispanic or Latino" ~ "Not Hispanic or Latino Black",
      ethnicity == "Hispanic or Latino" ~ "Hispanic or Latino",
      TRUE ~ "Not Hispanic or Latino Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(visit == "baseline") %>%
  relocate(all_of(lip), .after = last_col())


#keep all NA MRNs -- unique patients involved in only one study
na_mrn <- harm_lipids %>%
  filter(is.na(mrn))
  
#keep only one row per unqiue MRN--making sure patients only show up in one study
unique_mrn <- harm_lipids %>%
  filter(!is.na(mrn)) %>%
  distinct(mrn, .keep_all = TRUE)

#combined unique ids
harm_lipids <- bind_rows(na_mrn, unique_mrn)

#adding back in group info for attempt. For some reason it drops some of the Type 1 Diabetes group designations
harm_lipids <- harm_lipids %>%
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      grepl("ATTEMPT", study) & is.na(group) ~ "Type 1 Diabetes",
      TRUE ~ group
    )
  )

```


```{r}
#-----subsetting lipids dataframe--------
meta_lips <- harm_lipids %>% select(1:20) %>% as.data.frame()
rownames(meta_lips) <- meta_lips$record_id

#------adding rownames ------------------
lipids <- harm_lipids %>% select(-(1:20)) %>% as.data.frame()
rownames(lipids) <- meta_lips$record_id



#checking to see if any metadata was kept in lipids expression matrix or if any lipids are being read as characters

non_numeric2 <- names(lipids)[!vapply(lipids, is.numeric, logical(1))]
length(non_numeric2)
head(non_numeric2, 50)   # show first 50 offenders
```


```{r}
#----------------------------
#reading in the somascan file
#----------------------------

harm_soma<- read.csv("~/leidholt/Data/soma_harmonized_dataset.csv", na.strings ="")

```

```{r}
#filtering to final proteomics dataset
harm_proteins <- harm_soma %>%
  arrange(record_id) %>%
  filter(study %in% studies_keep) %>%
  select(any_of(c(id_cols, clin_cols)), starts_with("seq")) %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      where(~ !is.numeric(.x)),
      ~{
        vals <- unique(na.omit(.x))
        if (length(vals) == 0) NA_character_
        else if (length(vals) == 1) as.character(vals)
        else NA_character_
      }
    ),
    across(
      where(is.numeric),
      ~ if (all(is.na(.x))) NA_real_ else mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)
    ),
    .by = c(record_id, visit)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    race_ethnicity_condensed = case_when(
      race == "White" & ethnicity == "Not Hispanic or Latino" ~ "Not Hispanic or Latino White",
      race == "Black or African American" & ethnicity == "Not Hispanic or Latino" ~ "Not Hispanic or Latino Black",
      ethnicity == "Hispanic or Latino" ~ "Hispanic or Latino",
      TRUE ~ "Not Hispanic or Latino Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(visit == "baseline") %>%
  relocate(starts_with("seq"), .after = last_col())


#keep all NA MRNs -- unique patients involved in only one study
na_mrn <- harm_proteins %>%
  filter(is.na(mrn))
  
#keep only one row per unqiue MRN--making sure patients only show up in one study
unique_mrn <- harm_proteins%>%
  filter(!is.na(mrn)) %>%
  distinct(mrn, .keep_all = TRUE)

#combined unique ids
harm_proteins<- bind_rows(na_mrn, unique_mrn)
```


```{r}
#-----------------------------
#adding in attempt acr_u
#-----------------------------

harm_proteins <- harm_proteins %>%
  left_join(attempt_acr, by = c("record_id" = "subject_id", "visit" = "visit")) %>%
  mutate(
    acr_u = if_else(study == "ATTEMPT" & is.na(acr_u), acr_u_attempt, acr_u),
    group = if_else(study == "ATTEMPT", "Type 1 Diabetes", group)
  ) %>%
  select(-acr_u_attempt)

```

```{r}
# ---- CHECKPOINT A: do seq columns exist and have non-NA? ----
seq_cols <- grep("^seq", names(harm_proteins), value = TRUE)

c(
  n_rows = nrow(harm_proteins),
  n_seq_cols = length(seq_cols),
  nonNA_seq_total = sum(!is.na(harm_proteins[, seq_cols, drop = FALSE]))
)

# peek a few values (do you REALLY see numbers here?)
harm_proteins[1:3, head(seq_cols, 3)]
```


```{r}
# ---- split metadata vs analytes ----

meta_prot <- harm_proteins %>% select(1:20) %>% as.data.frame()
rownames(meta_prot) <- meta_prot$record_id

combined <- harm_proteins %>% select(-(1:20)) %>% as.data.frame()
rownames(combined) <- meta_prot$record_id

# urine proteins end in "...urine" OR "...urine_cradj"
prot_urine <- combined %>%
  select(matches("(urine|urine_cradj)$"))

# plasma proteins = remaining seq columns
prot_plasma <- combined %>%
  select(-matches("(urine|urine_cradj)$"))

# ---- set rownames consistently ----
prot_urine <- as.data.frame(prot_urine)
rownames(prot_urine) <- meta_prot$record_id

prot_plasma <- as.data.frame(prot_plasma)
rownames(prot_plasma) <- meta_prot$record_id

#checking to see if any metadata remains in final prot_plasma matrix

non_numeric3 <- names(prot_plasma)[!vapply(prot_plasma, is.numeric, logical(1))]
length(non_numeric3)
head(non_numeric3, 50) 

# ---- sanity checks ----
stopifnot(all(grepl("(urine|urine_cradj)$", colnames(prot_urine))))
stopifnot(!any(grepl("(urine|urine_cradj)$", colnames(prot_plasma))))

dim(prot_urine)
dim(prot_plasma)


table(meta_prot$group, useNA = "ifany")
table(meta_prot$study, meta_prot$group, useNA = "ifany")

n_table <- meta_prot %>%
  count(study, group) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = group,
              values_from = n,
              values_fill = 0) %>%
  arrange(study)

n_table
```


```{r}

# ---- save for other Rmd ----
out_path <- "/mmfs1/gscratch/togo/leidholt/Data/R_objects/omics_split_objects3.rds"

saveRDS(
  list(
    meta_prot = meta_prot,
    prot_plasma = prot_plasma,
    prot_urine = prot_urine,
    meta_lips = meta_lips,
    lipids = lipids,
    meta_metab = meta_metab,
    metabolites = metabolites,
    n_table = n_table
  ),
  file = out_path
)

out_path


```

```{r}
# =========================================================
# Combine metabolomics + lipidomics + proteomics (union IDs)
# NO feature prefixes, NO metadata suffixes
# =========================================================

# --- inputs assumed to already exist ---
# meta_metab, metabolites
# meta_lips,  lipids
# meta_prot,  prot_plasma, prot_urine

# -----------------------------
# 0) helpers
# -----------------------------
make_unique_ids <- function(x) {
  # if record_id duplicates exist, make them unique (rare but safe)
  if (anyDuplicated(x)) make.unique(as.character(x)) else as.character(x)
}

fix_rownames_to_record_id <- function(meta_df, mat_df = NULL, id_col = "record_id") {
  meta_df <- as.data.frame(meta_df)

  # ensure id_col exists as a column
  if (!(id_col %in% names(meta_df))) {
    meta_df[[id_col]] <- rownames(meta_df)
  }
  meta_df[[id_col]] <- make_unique_ids(meta_df[[id_col]])

  # set rownames on meta
  rownames(meta_df) <- meta_df[[id_col]]

  # if a matrix provided, align it to meta by rownames and set rownames too
  if (!is.null(mat_df)) {
    mat_df <- as.data.frame(mat_df)

    # if mat has no rownames, set from meta
    if (is.null(rownames(mat_df))) rownames(mat_df) <- rownames(meta_df)

    # reorder matrix rows to match meta rows (drop=FALSE keeps data.frame)
    mat_df <- mat_df[rownames(meta_df), , drop = FALSE]
    rownames(mat_df) <- rownames(meta_df)
  }

  list(meta = meta_df, mat = mat_df)
}

align_to_ids <- function(df, ids) {
  df <- as.data.frame(df)
  df <- df[match(ids, rownames(df)), , drop = FALSE]
  rownames(df) <- ids
  df
}

fill_to_ids <- function(mat, ids) {
  mat <- as.data.frame(mat)
  out <- matrix(
    NA_real_,
    nrow = length(ids),
    ncol = ncol(mat),
    dimnames = list(ids, colnames(mat))
  )
  overlap <- intersect(ids, rownames(mat))
  out[overlap, ] <- as.matrix(mat[overlap, , drop = FALSE])
  as.data.frame(out)
}

coalesce3 <- function(a, b, c) dplyr::coalesce(a, b, c)

# -----------------------------
# 1) standardize rownames everywhere
# -----------------------------
metab_fixed <- fix_rownames_to_record_id(meta_metab, metabolites, id_col = "record_id")
meta_metab2 <- metab_fixed$meta
metab_mat   <- metab_fixed$mat

lip_fixed   <- fix_rownames_to_record_id(meta_lips, lipids, id_col = "record_id")
meta_lips2  <- lip_fixed$meta
lipid_mat   <- lip_fixed$mat

# proteomics: meta_prot governs both plasma+urine
prot_fixed  <- fix_rownames_to_record_id(meta_prot, NULL, id_col = "record_id")
meta_prot2  <- prot_fixed$meta

# enforce proteomics matrices follow meta_prot row order
protP_mat <- as.data.frame(prot_plasma)[rownames(meta_prot2), , drop = FALSE]
protU_mat <- as.data.frame(prot_urine)[rownames(meta_prot2), , drop = FALSE]
rownames(protP_mat) <- rownames(meta_prot2)
rownames(protU_mat) <- rownames(meta_prot2)

# sanity checks
stopifnot(
  identical(rownames(meta_metab2), rownames(metab_mat)),
  identical(rownames(meta_lips2),  rownames(lipid_mat)),
  identical(rownames(meta_prot2),  rownames(protP_mat)),
  identical(rownames(meta_prot2),  rownames(protU_mat))
)

# -----------------------------
# 2) union IDs (allow missing blocks)
# -----------------------------
ids <- Reduce(union, list(
  rownames(metab_mat),
  rownames(lipid_mat),
  rownames(meta_prot2)
))

# -----------------------------
# 3) Build meta_all WITHOUT suffixes (coalesce across sources)
# -----------------------------
meta_cols_keep <- c(
  "record_id", "mrn", "study", "visit", "group", "sex", "race", "ethnicity",
  "age", "bmi", "eGFR_fas_cr", "hba1c", "acr_u",
  "race_ethnicity_condensed"
)


# align each meta to union ids
mp <- align_to_ids(meta_prot2,  ids)
ml <- align_to_ids(meta_lips2,  ids)
mm <- align_to_ids(meta_metab2, ids)

# ensure record_id exists
if (!("record_id" %in% names(mp))) mp$record_id <- rownames(mp)
if (!("record_id" %in% names(ml))) ml$record_id <- rownames(ml)
if (!("record_id" %in% names(mm))) mm$record_id <- rownames(mm)

# --------------------------------------
# ADD meta_numeric HERE (before the loop)
# --------------------------------------
meta_numeric <- c(
  "age", "bmi", "eGFR_fas_cr", "hba1c", "acr_u"
)

# initialize
meta_all <- as.data.frame(matrix(NA, nrow = length(ids), ncol = 0))
rownames(meta_all) <- ids

# --------------------------------------
# THEN your coalescing loop
# --------------------------------------
for (cc in meta_cols_keep) {
  a <- if (cc %in% names(mp)) mp[[cc]] else rep(NA, length(ids))
  b <- if (cc %in% names(ml)) ml[[cc]] else rep(NA, length(ids))
  c <- if (cc %in% names(mm)) mm[[cc]] else rep(NA, length(ids))

  if (cc %in% meta_numeric) {
    # force numeric consistency across datasets
    a <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(a)))
    b <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(b)))
    c <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(c)))
    meta_all[[cc]] <- dplyr::coalesce(a, b, c)
  } else {
    # force character consistency (handles factors safely)
    a <- as.character(a)
    b <- as.character(b)
    c <- as.character(c)
    meta_all[[cc]] <- dplyr::coalesce(a, b, c)
  }
}

meta_all$record_id <- ids
rownames(meta_all) <- meta_all$record_id
```

```{r}
X_all <- cbind(
  fill_to_ids(metab_mat, ids),
  fill_to_ids(lipid_mat, ids),
  fill_to_ids(protP_mat, ids),
  fill_to_ids(protU_mat, ids)
)

stopifnot(identical(rownames(meta_all), rownames(X_all)))

omics_wide <- cbind(meta_all, X_all)
```

```{r}
dim(omics_wide)

# -----------------------------
# 6) Save
# -----------------------------
saveRDS(
  omics_wide,
  "/mmfs1/gscratch/togo/leidholt/Data/R_objects/omics_wide_union_baseline.rds"
)
```
